arts.ttl: Real Madrid’s TRIPLE injury blow, Man City RECORD defeat, Chelsea’s Kepa LOSS
arts.sum: Real Madrid ’s transfer plans for January have changed after they were rocked by a fresh injury blow, Manchester City have slipped further off the Premier League pace behind Liverpool and Chelsea are bracing themselves for a huge loss. As a result, Real Madrid are set to make moves in the January transfer window, with Fabrizio Romano confirming their interest in Trent Alexander-Arnold of Liverpool and Castello Lukeba of RB Leipzig.

arts.ttl: Guardiola faces his biggest Man City challenge yet
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola walked into his press conference after Manchester City's 2-1 defeat at Brighton and was asked if he now faces his biggest challenge at the club.

arts.ttl: Why Pep Guardiola proteges Mikel Arteta and Enzo Maresca will NOT replace him at Manchester City, writes DANNY MURPHY
arts.sum: It's a massive compliment to Pep Guardiola that two of the brightest Premier League managers worked for him at Manchester City and have greatly improved Chelsea and Arsenal on what they learned.

arts.ttl: Brighton 2-1 Man City: Fabian Hurzeler claims "important" Premier League win is 'not the biggest' of his career
arts.sum: Brighton boss Fabian Hurzeler heaps praise on his side's "important" 2-1 victory over Man City in the Premier League, but he does not believe it is the biggest win of his managerial career.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola makes brutally honest title admission after setting unwanted Man City record
arts.sum: Manchester City suffered a fourth straight defeat in all competitions on Saturday, losing 2-1 at Brighton to give Premier League title rivals Liverpool a welcome boost

arts.ttl: Man City's next six fixtures compared to Liverpool, Arsenal and Chelsea amid latest title race twist
arts.sum: Manchester City were beaten at Brighton on Saturday before Liverpool defeated Aston Villa to move five points clear at the top of the Premier League table

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola makes major Premier League title admission after record-breaking Man City loss
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola admits that another Premier League team may 'deserve' to win the title this season after his Man City side lost 2-1 away against Brighton - the club's fourth loss in a row across all competitions.

arts.ttl: Liverpool 2-0 Aston Villa: Reds go FIVE points clear of Man City as Darwin Nunez bags much-needed goal and Mohamed Salah steals the show - but Trent Alexander-Arnold comes off injured
arts.sum: JOE BERNSTEIN AT ANFIELD: Liverpool went five points clear at the top of the Premier League when Nunez completed the perfect Saturday night breakaway - from an Aston Villa corner.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola says Man City cannot maintain high level but vows ‘we’ll be back’
arts.sum: City suffered a fourth successive defeat after going down 2-1 at Brighto

arts.ttl: I have to solve this, says Guardiola after Manchester City lose fourth in a row
arts.athr: Ed Aarons at the Amex Stadium
arts.sum: Manager insists return of injured players will be key He believes ‘the people’ want City to keep losing Pep Guardiola said it was up to him to find a solution to Manchester City’s malaise after suffering a fourth successive defeat for the first time in his managerial career. Goals from the substitutes João Pedro and Matt O’Riley capped a superb comeback from Brighton after Erling Haaland had put City ahead in the first half. Guardiola’s side were beaten 2-1 at Bournemouth after being knocked out of the EFL Cup at Tottenham and were then thrashed 4-1 in the Champions League on Tuesday by Sporting in Lisbon.
